qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Twig SSTI/RCE via unsandboxed element ID template rendering in relational condition rules
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a server-side template injection vulnerability in BaseElementSelectConditionRule via Twig template rendering, tracked under a different GHSA (GHSA-fp5j-j7j4-mcxc). The advisory describes blind SQL injection in ElementSearchController::actionSearch() due to missing unset() protection. These are different vulnerable controllers, different vulnerability classes (SSTI vs SQL injection), different root causes, and different attack vectors.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes blind SQL injection in CraftCMS's ElementSearchController::actionSearch() endpoint due to missing unset() protection for criteria parameters. The 'twig-ssti-rce-condition-rules' finding describes SSTI/RCE via condition rule template rendering, which references a different GHSA (GHSA-fp5j-j7j4-mcxc) and a different vulnerable component (BaseElementSelectConditionRule and View::renderObjectTemplate). The finding does not mention ElementSearchController, criteria[where], criteria[orderBy], or the missing unset() protection that characterizes the advisory vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory specifies ElementSearchController::actionSearch() as the vulnerable component
Advisory states the vulnerability is missing unset() protection for criteria parameters that was added to ElementIndexesController in GHSA-2453-mppf-46cj
Advisory describes SQL injection via criteria[where], criteria[orderBy], or other query properties
The 'twig-ssti-rce-condition-rules' finding describes SSTI in BaseElementSelectConditionRule via Twig template rendering, explicitly referencing a different GHSA (GHSA-fp5j-j7j4-mcxc)
